noveltyJustification: This pass converted the prior broad system-spec-kit coupling observation into an ownership taxonomy: external governance, narrow seam re-exports, path-rewritten external consumers, and dev-tool dependency hygiene.
keyQuestions: Which system-spec-kit tooling should be borrowed, wrapped, or left external instead of duplicated inside the merged deep-loop system?
answeredQuestions: system-spec-kit should keep ownership of spec-folder topology, validation, templates, memory/context save, and canonical resource-map extraction; system-deep-loop should consume these through narrow seams and update external consumer configs, not duplicate the tooling.

ruledOut.approach: Duplicate spec-kit validation, memory save, or resource-map extraction inside system-deep-loop
ruledOut.reason: Would split canonical spec-folder governance and continuity ownership.

label: Spec validation and memory continuity stay external
Artifact topology remains single-source via runtime seam
Resource-map rendering is workflow synthesis, not runtime plumbing
Runtime still borrows narrow dev tooling from system-spec-kit node_modules

direction: Duplicate system-spec-kit governance tooling inside system-deep-loop
reason: Spec-folder validation, continuity, memory save, and canonical templates are system-spec-kit ownership, not deep-loop runtime ownership.
